aiChat.rule: Every development agent reads recent chat messages before making project changes.
Every development agent posts a start message before making project changes.
The Orchestrator or Producer creates Task Queue items before roles do non-read-only implementation work.
Execution roles do not edit project files, deploy, or create runtime assets without an assigned or claimed task for their role.
Agents may read, ask clarifying questions, report blockers, and post concise Idea messages without claiming a task.

Before non-trivial multi-role or shared-file work, agents agree a Parallel Plan in chat with workstreams, owners, exact file scopes, branch or task id, dependencies, merge order, and validation owner.
Agents do not edit outside their agreed Parallel Plan scope until the Orchestrator, Producer, or affected roles acknowledge the split.
Every development agent reports meaningful changes and validation results to chat.

Every role occasionally proposes concise actionable Idea messages for project development when a concrete opportunity appears, without spamming, repeating ideas, or exceeding one idea per substantial work block unless the Producer asks.
Messages include date, self-declared role, current project version, branch, and commit when available.
Commits from branches are shown in the separate service menu, not mixed into chat messages.

When GitHub CLI or connector access is unavailable, repository settings and webhooks may be managed through the logged-in Codex in-app Browser session; browser-driven GitHub changes must be reported in /ai_chat and verified through webhook delivery evidence or production health.

Primary autodeploy is triggered by a signed GitHub push webhook. Moscow may also be triggered by the primary-to-Moscow deploy relay or by the Moscow fallback timer while GitHub-side hook creation is blocked.

Webhook deploys must expose the latest result in /ai_chat/api/deploy-health.last_deploy, queue the newest main push while another deploy is running, and fail visibly if an autodeploy lock cannot be acquired.
Telegram bridge uses /ai_chat/api/telegram-webhook, accepts only the configured group, and relays real-user messages as Producer messages.
Telegram bot token and chat id are stored only in the server environment file.

taskQueue.rule: Tasks include role, priority, title, description, file scope, dependencies, acceptance, owner or claim, status, comments, and validation owner.
Only Producer or Orchestrator creates, seeds, or re-scopes tasks.

subordination.role: Execution role with voice and warning rights.
Execution role with voice and warning rights.
Execution role with voice and warning rights.
Execution role with voice and warning rights.

repositoryHygiene.rule: Every agent runs git status --short --branch before changes and before finishing.
No staged files may be left between tasks; staging is only for an immediate intentional commit.

Never stage generated package zips, caches, logs, temp outputs, local data, secrets, service env files, ai_chat/data/, __pycache__/, or *.pyc. UnSoccer client/server/shared dist artifacts are allowed only for intentional fast production releases.
Accidental staged files are unstaged without reverting file content.
Unrelated user or agent changes are preserved and reported, not reverted.
Final git cleanliness is reported in /ai_chat after meaningful work.

artDirection.rule: Use art_director/ for non-runtime art-direction audits, references, tests, drafts, prompts, and QA evidence.
Art Director occasionally posts concise Idea messages for project development when a concrete visual opportunity appears, without constant idea spam.
Do not place shipped game code or final game assets in art_director/.

workspace.name: ui_designer
workspace.rule: Use ui_designer/ for non-runtime UI audits, flows, wireframes, copy, references, and viewport checks.
UI Designer owns local public pages, game entry presentation, itch.io page direction, screenshots, captions, metadata, and catalog cards.
UI Designer occasionally posts concise Idea messages for project development when a concrete opportunity appears, without constant idea spam.
Do not place shipped game code or final UI assets in ui_designer/.

workspace.name: game_designer
workspace.rule: Use game_designer/ for non-runtime ideas, systems, tuning, level notes, playtests, and briefs.
Game Designer proposes creative ideas and feature improvements when a concrete opportunity improves the current task, without forcing constant idea posts.
Game Designer posts project-development ideas to chat as concise Idea messages, but avoids repeated or constant idea posting unless the Producer asks.

workspace.name: programmer
workspace.rule: Use programmer/ for non-runtime investigations, profiling, compatibility notes, plans, and validation checks.
Programmer occasionally posts concise Idea messages for project development when a concrete technical opportunity appears, without constant idea spam.
Do not import programmer/ files into the game runtime.

workspace.name: tester
workspace.rule: Use tester/ for non-runtime plans, bug notes, evidence, playtests, regression checks, and creative QA ideas.
Tester must keep bugs separate from creative feature and polish suggestions.
Tester occasionally posts concise Idea messages for project development when testing reveals a concrete opportunity, without constant idea spam.

workspace.name: sound_designer
workspace.rule: Use sound_designer/ for non-runtime source research, license notes, generation prompts, raw sounds, temp edits, mix notes, and audio checks.
Sound Designer may search free sounds or generate sounds, then implement approved audio into the game with documented provenance.
Sound Designer occasionally posts concise Idea messages for project development when a concrete audio opportunity appears, without constant idea spam.

workspace.name: ai_chat
workspace.rule: Use ai_chat/ for the shared development-agent chat service code, static UI, deployment references, and server-only message storage.

tooling.rule: Generated package output belongs under root dist/ and is not committed.
UnSoccer client/server/shared dist artifacts may be committed only for intentional fast production release deploys.
Use git config core.hooksPath tools/hooks in this clone so the tracked post-commit hook owns automatic server mirror deployment.
Set ITCH_IO_TARGET=owner/game:channel to let the post-commit hook publish UnSoccer to itch.io after production is ready.
Packaging fails if internal skill, role, agent coordination, deploy, or source-control docs leak into the public zip.

game.name: unsoccer
game.title: Ragdoll Soccer II

assetRules.rule: Free3D authenticated downloads require FREE3D_API_TOKEN/F3D_API_TOKEN and exact format/lod/relativePath tuples.
Tokenless Free3D LowPoly 1k assets may use exact public worker inventory paths.json relativePath entries with provenance.

Runtime 3D assets must be local optimized textureless files with provenance before shipping: no image texture files in client/public/assets/{characters,environment,balls}, no GLB images/textures, no material texture references, no FBX image references, and baked COLOR_0 vertex colors. Character GLBs must also include packed TEXCOORD_1 roughness/metalness. Deleting textures without baking vertex color is not acceptable.
Character runtime roster entries must include a local textureless GLB and FBX idle/walk/run/jump clips before their ids are added to shared/src/index.ts CHARACTER_ROSTER.

AutoRig task character entries must not ship embedded-texture animations.glb in the runtime roster until converted to textureless vertex/PBR assets; record locked bundle/download status in provenance.
Environment runtime GLBs live under client/public/assets/environment/free3d/ with source provenance under assets/models/environment/free3d/provenance.json.

game.rule: First 4 connected clients are players; clients 5-32 are spectators/testers.
Itch package is static client-only and requires a separately deployed multiplayer server.

Production transport is plain WebSocket JSON under /unsoccer/socket/ with HTTP polling fallback and built-in Node http/net/crypto handling; geckos.io, ws, and native node-datachannel must not be required runtime dependencies on the Qwertystock host.
v0.0.008 keeps the HUD version and client bundle weight visible and starts the residential courtyard visual pass around the field.
v0.0.008 server audioEvents synchronize roster, kick/body, goal, and countdown cues across WebSocket and HTTP fallback clients by authoritative event id.
v0.0.009 ships the Art Director runtime 3D/animation pass: expanded courtyard models, animated procedural footballer rigs, visible sun/moon/orbit marker, and 120-second lighting datasets.
v0.0.009 fixes team-relative WASD movement, immediate local controlled-player rendering, and removes artificial pitch bounds from player movement.

v0.0.009 ships the UI Designer runtime pass: redesigned HUD, event/status/network panels, toolbar, settings modal tabs, localStorage settings, remappable input, audio/graphics/network/accessibility controls, and QA-readable UI datasets.

v0.0.010 is the unified 0010 release build with all current runtime workstreams, a camera-framed sun/moon marker, synchronized version surfaces, stable network HUD numeric columns, and repo-side publication evidence.

v0.0.011 doubles the field/courtyard, starts authoritative time at 06:00, rotates sun/moon by server day time, randomizes weather every 60-120s, adds dawn birds/day traffic audio, moving cars, sprint/stamina/exhaustion/jump, foot/hand/head player hits, bouncier ball physics, thicker goal rebounds, local cloth net ripples, and 10 local Free3D vertex-color soccer-ball GLBs.

v0.0.015 extracts the Free3D skinned character runtime into a reusable controller, adds a standalone controller test page, blends idle/walk/run/jump by velocity with hysteresis/time scaling, applies procedural bone-IK overlays for foot/hand/head/body/jump strikes, extends the authoritative day cycle to 300 seconds, shortens true dark hours to 23:00-03:00, and adds night-only floodlight masts with local volumetric beam meshes.

v0.0.016 halves goal post and crossbar radius, switches posts to neutral light metal, scores goals only on front goal-line plane crossing from the field side, rejects back-net entries, and adds clear center-circle, center-spot, penalty-box, goal-area, and penalty-spot markings.
v0.0.017 adds the local stamina HUD meter with state colors/labels, exposes local stamina datasets, and makes hand hits readable with a longer orange strike trail plus hand-action datasets.

v0.0.017 character input/strike correction uses middle mouse button for head hits, prevents mouse wheel from triggering attacks, sends foot/hand ball impulses forward along player yaw from the limb-specific contact point, and alternates hand-hit visual/contact side without adding a new network KickKind.
v0.0.018 keeps the camera anchored to the controlled player with smoothed velocity lead, removes ball-driven camera drift, and adds HUD offscreen arrows for the ball and other players.

v0.0.035 textureless asset correction keeps the local runtime roster to 10 Free3D skinned characters with baked textureless vertex/PBR GLB plus FBX clips, removes the embedded-texture AutoRig entry from server assignment, and makes acceptance/package fail on runtime texture files, textured GLBs, or missing baked COLOR_0 on GLB primitives.
v0.0.019 extends local-only goal nets into closed cages with back, roof, left-side, and right-side Verlet panels per goal while keeping net motion off the network.
v0.0.020 halves the authoritative shared BALL_RADIUS from 0.48 to 0.24 so the server collider, visible client ball, Free3D ball scale, sideline rack, and acceptance fixtures stay in sync.
v0.0.021 delays goal reset: 5 seconds of scoring-team celebration, then a 1-second server-authored ball return to kickoff before countdown; never teleport the ball to center immediately after a goal.
v0.0.021 balances the half-size ball with BALL_DENSITY 3.6 and lower normal hit impulses; acceptance guards against runaway projectile speeds.

v0.0.022 retargets Free3D character hand-strike IK so alternating hand hits keep logical left/right side, compensate mirrored roster arm bones, and punch forward at upper-chest/shoulder height with matching raised hand flash/trail.

v0.0.022 smooths authoritative keyboard movement: raw axes ramp, released axes decay, opposite axes take over faster, and controlled velocity accelerates/brakes; client prediction mirrors this and exposes data-movement-smoothing, data-local-move-speed, and data-local-move-axis for QA.
v0.0.023 assigns newly joined players random ready characters from CHARACTER_ROSTER through a shuffled non-repeating server deck and preserves player.characterId during role/team rebalancing.
v0.0.024 uses team-colored ground indicator rings and halos under players; browser QA exposes data-local-team-marker and data-local-team-marker-color for the local player.

v0.0.024 adds jumpRun for sprint/high-velocity jumps; until dedicated run-jump FBX clips are added to the runtime roster, jumpRun clones the normal jump clip and applies a stronger forward-leap IK overlay exposed through data-player-rig-jump-style=run.

v0.0.025 follows a lerped authoritative player offset for camera position/look target instead of skinned mesh or bone transforms; browser QA exposes data-camera-anchor-smoothing, data-camera-anchor-offset, and data-camera-follow-speed.

v0.0.025 activates server-authored player.ragdoll/ragdollAt when stamina reaches zero: sprint exhaustion preserves movement inertia, stamina-emptying hits apply heavy knockback/lift, local prediction must not override ragdoll snapshots, and browser QA exposes data-player-rig-ragdoll plus data-local-player-ragdoll.

v0.0.026 widens night floodlight coverage with broader SpotLight beams, larger volumetric cones, per-mast white-temperature variation, and subtle deterministic flicker exposed through data-stadium-light-beam-angle, data-stadium-light-beam-radius, data-stadium-light-palette, and data-stadium-light-flicker.

v0.0.027 makes player-ball contact height-aware: body bumps require vertical body overlap, foot/hand/head hits require vertical reach, jumps can clear ground balls, and unreachable high balls cannot be headed.

v0.0.028 extends the residential courtyard through client/src/environment-props.ts with 100+ environment instances, 8 local Free3D GLB environment asset sources, and QA datasets for environment/free3d instance counts.

v0.0.029 doubles ordinary foot/hand/head ball-hit power and adds server-authored LMB left-foot charge from 2x tap power to 4x full power over one second; held charge may fire once on ball contact before release and browser QA exposes data-local-kick-charge.
v0.0.029 widens gameplay camera framing and adds close sideline pennant/bench strips so the dense v0.0.028 courtyard dressing is visible in normal play while the pitch remains clear.

v0.0.030 makes striking more user-friendly: foot/hand/head ball hits have player-centered assist reach, LMB clicks buffer for a short contact window, active strike inputs suppress same-frame body bump, and body-only ball contact is smaller, softer, and less frequent.

v0.0.031 adds server-authoritative bots that fill active player slots to four, give human/test clients active-slot priority, do not count as connectedClients, expose controller=bot snapshots, use the existing movement/combat/scoring rules, and can be tuned through /api/bot-settings plus the static bot tuning page.

v0.0.032 uses unsoccer/game-settings.json as the runtime tuning source, exposes GET/POST /api/game-settings plus POST /api/game-settings/reload, sends ServerState.settings/settingsRevision to clients, and ships unsoccer/client/public/game-admin.html as the compact schema-driven admin page while keeping game-settings.html as the larger legacy admin surface. New UnSoccer features with tunable gameplay/world/bot/audio/camera/lighting/prop/UI behavior must update GameSettings, DEFAULT_GAME_SETTINGS, GAME_SETTINGS_SCHEMA, game-settings.json, and the admin page contract in the same change. The settings admin is Russian-facing, GAME_SETTINGS_SCHEMA must cover every JSON/defaults key, and acceptance must prove per-key apply/readback through /api/game-settings.

v0.0.033 adds player communication and personalization: a local-only 9-emotion mouse-wheel selector above the local player, replicated applied emotions in PlayerSnapshot.emotion, compact bottom-right chat, profile controls for nickname/skin/userPic across WebSocket and HTTP fallback, and server-authored body-side combat metadata where hand punches alternate right/left and LMB foot kicks use the current trailingFoot exposed through lastActionSide/trailingFoot/stancePhase.

v0.0.036+ stamina drains only from Shift sprint and incoming player-hit damage. Space jump, LMB/RMB attacks, and whiffs must never spend attacker stamina or block recovery; playerStaminaJumpCost/playerStaminaHitCost stay clamped to zero in settings.

v0.0.040 exposes bot runtime health through /api/health, /api/game-settings, /api/bot-settings, and browser QA datasets: active bot count, visible bot count, hidden active player count, runtime bot enablement, target fill, human clients, and test-mode state must be inspectable without guessing.

v0.0.041 moves HTTP fallback stale-player cleanup into runtime settings as httpClientStaleMs, exposes it through /api/health, and ragdolls zero-stamina non-ragdolled player-hit targets with hit knockback instead of letting them keep walking.

v0.0.042 adds websocketClientStaleMs to runtime settings and health, removes frozen WebSocket clients before bot fill, exposes desiredBotPlayers/nonBotActiveSlots/botFillSuppressionReason, and lets the client retry normal connection flow after WebSocket disconnect when auto-reconnect is enabled.

v0.0.043 makes off-ball fighting more reliable: RMB/head input counters are consumed only after an accepted strike or visible action, airborne LMB dash contributes swept player-hit reach when it misses the ball, and stamina still drains only from Shift plus incoming full-hit damage.

v0.0.044 makes bot fill self-heal during normal server ticks, requires bot combat to apply the same one-hit ragdoll knockout as human combat, and makes possessed balls drop ownership and rebound when they collide with another player.

v0.0.049 hardens the release gate for stamina, combat, and bots: acceptance must assert default friendly fire, one-hit knockout, zero jump cost, zero attacker hit cost, free head whiffs, and a separate non-test local server with testMode=false plus unsuppressed ten-bot fill.

v0.0.050 makes WebSocket leave/backfill explicit: browser pagehide sends /api/leave for WebSocket and HTTP players, /api/leave removes WebSocket players and closes their channel, and default websocketClientStaleMs is 12000 so stale human slots stop hiding bots quickly. Airborne LMB dash-kicks have wider swept player-hit reach for reliable jump knockouts. Exhausted standing players cannot own/capture the ball; high-aggression bots can finish exhausted opponents into ragdoll, while default bot combat pressure stays gated above the default aggression so bot-only football matches do not collapse.

v0.0.051 lets default-aggression bots visibly brawl again without breaking bot fill. botCombatAggressionThreshold, botCombatCollapseGuardRatio, and botCombatCollapseGuardMinDisabled live in game-settings.json and the admin schema, and acceptance proves one-hit bot knockout, stable bot ids, active bot fill, and no fill suppression.

v0.0.052 fixes point-blank no-ball strikes so overlap-range LMB/RMB contacts apply the same one-hit stamina knockout and ragdoll knockback as visible strikes. Acceptance also validates active bot ids, roles, finite playable-area positions, non-test human join backfill, and LMB+Shift possession shots with Shift remaining a strong-shot modifier.
v0.0.054 mirrors orange-team left/right input in client code while preserving the current blue-team mapping and without changing the controls GUI.
v0.0.053 keeps the v0.0.052 gameplay contract and adds an itch.io/itch.zone hosted-build transport fallback to the production WebSocket/API endpoints.

v0.0.055 makes /admin a first-class local runtime-settings entry, defaults botTargetActivePlayers to 3, and lowers kickRange/footKickAssistRange/handKickAssistRange/headKickAssistRange to reduce too-distant ball strike triggers while keeping all values adjustable through the Russian admin schema.

v0.0.056 strips stale Free3D FBX image references from character animation clips, removes the transparent data-image fallback, rejects runtime texture maps in character/environment/ball loaders, and makes acceptance/package fail on texture files, GLB texture slots, or FBX image refs.
Local browser play servers for UnSoccer must run without UNSOCCER_TEST_MODE; that flag is only for isolated acceptance and prevents normal default bot fill unless /api/test/bots enables it.
tools/unsoccer_acceptance.mjs derives the expected version from package.json.games.unsoccer.version and checks HTTP fallback join/input/state.
npm run package:unsoccer rebuilds before writing dist/unsoccer-itch.zip so stale dist output cannot become the release package.

staticServing.rule: Each game keeps a public static directory with relative links so nginx can map /game-name/ without backend static routes.

publicSurfaces.rule: Itch upload packages must exclude internal skill, role, agent coordination, server, and source-control docs.

Public game pages, clients, screenshots, and assets are static bundles served directly by nginx from each game public directory; do not add app-server routes for static games or assets unless the Producer explicitly asks for dynamic behavior.
Orbital Courier itch upload must include vendored Three.js and must not require cdn.jsdelivr.net at runtime.
External itch.io pages remain incomplete until the public URL, uploaded zip, exact live copy, screenshots, publication date, and version match are recorded under ui_designer/public_pages/.

versioning.rule: Every created game starts at v0.0.001.
Every shipped bump increments the version.
The current version is visible in the bottom-left corner.
Version bumps are committed, pushed to GitHub, and autodeployed.
